title: Finite Calibration And Brier Score

solver_reuse.target: finite calibration bad Brier-score QF_LRA/Farkas regression seed
solver_reuse.pressure: Exact finite probabilistic-classifier replay computes Brier score 71/300 from six rational forecasts, while a malformed row claims 1/5, isolating a compact scalar Farkas conflict.
solver_reuse.next_step: Promote additional calibration or scoring-rule rows only when they add distinct binning-policy, multiclass, proper-scoring-rule, confidence-interval, or numerical-honesty pressure; keep statistical generalization and floating-point classifier claims in theorem or numerical-honesty lanes.

graduation_criteria: Encode the fixed finite probability forecasts, class counts, calibration bins, expected calibration error, and exact rational Brier score as deterministic replay obligations.
Route the false Brier-score claim through a source-linked QF_LRA artifact and checked Farkas evidence after exact replay computes the conflicting Brier equation.
Expose the pack through probability, statistics, finite classifier, calibration/Brier, exact-vs-floating, and learner-query surfaces.

Keep binning policy, calibration theory, scoring-rule optimality, confidence intervals, statistical generalization, continuous score-distribution theory, and floating-point classifier behavior marked theorem or numerical-honesty horizon.
